DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_data;
CREATE TABLE test_data(population Float64) ENGINE = Log;
INSERT INTO test_data SELECT randUniform(5.5, 10) FROM numbers(1000000);
SELECT stddevPopStable(population) AS stddev FROM test_data;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_data_small;
CREATE TABLE test_data_small(population Float64) ENGINE = Log;
INSERT INTO test_data_small VALUES (5.5), (6.0), (6.5), (7.0), (7.5);
SELECT stddevPopStable(population) AS stddev FROM test_data_small;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_data_empty;
CREATE TABLE test_data_empty(population Float64) ENGINE = Log;
SELECT stddevPopStable(population) AS stddev FROM test_data_empty;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_data_negative;
CREATE TABLE test_data_negative(population Float64) ENGINE = Log;
INSERT INTO test_data_negative VALUES (-10.0), (-5.0), (-1.0), (0.0), (1.0);
SELECT stddevPopStable(population) AS stddev FROM test_data_negative;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_data_joined;
CREATE TABLE test_data_joined(id UInt32, population Float64) ENGINE = Log;
INSERT INTO test_data_joined VALUES (1, 8.0), (2, 9.0), (3, 7.5), (4, 6.0);
CREATE TABLE additional_data(id UInt32, factor Float64) ENGINE = Log;
INSERT INTO additional_data VALUES (1, 1.0), (2, 2.0), (3, 1.5), (4, 2.5);
SELECT stddevPopStable(d.population * a.factor) AS stddev FROM test_data_joined AS d JOIN additional_data AS a ON d.id = a.id;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_data_nested;
CREATE TABLE test_data_nested(population Float64) ENGINE = Log;
INSERT INTO test_data_nested SELECT randUniform(5.5, 10) FROM numbers(100);
SELECT stddevPopStable((SELECT AVG(population) FROM test_data_nested)) AS avg_stddev FROM dual;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_data_complex;
CREATE TABLE test_data_complex(group_id UInt32, population Float64) ENGINE = Log;
INSERT INTO test_data_complex VALUES (1, 5), (1, 6), (2, 9), (2, 10);
SELECT group_id, stddevPopStable(population) AS stddev FROM test_data_complex GROUP BY group_id;
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS test_data_combined;
CREATE TABLE test_data_combined(id UInt32, values Float64) ENGINE = Log;
INSERT INTO test_data_combined VALUES (1, 5.0), (2, 7.0), (3, 8.0), (4, 10.0);
SELECT stddevPopStable(values) AS combined_stddev FROM test_data_combined UNION ALL SELECT stddevPopStable(population) AS combined_stddev FROM test_data WHERE population > 5.0;
